title: Dilly Courgette & Halloumi Salad on Yoghurt
summary: Golden halloumi, smoky courgettes and a zingy lemon-dill dressing, all piled onto creamy Greek yoghurt. It’s salty, crunchy and seriously satisfying. Oh, and it only take 15 mins to make!

method.description: Drain the butter beans and set aside.

Slice the courgettes into 2cm-thick coins. Place a large frying pan over a high heat. Once hot, add the courgettes, cut-side down, and cook for 4-5 mins, flipping halfway through, until charred on both sides. Season with salt, then tip into a large bowl.

Meanwhile, split the halloumi down the crease and chop into 2cm chunks. Add to the same pan with a drizzle of olive oil and fry for 3-4 mins until golden on all sides. Tip into the bowl with the courgettes.

Quarter the beets and roughly chop the dill fronds. Add both to the bowl along with the butter beans. Zest the lemon into the bowl, squeeze in the juice and add a generous glug of olive oil. Toss everything together until well coated.
Spoon the Greek yoghurt onto the base of a plate and spread it out. Pile the courgette and halloumi salad over the top and serve.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
